title: COSO Principle 16 — Ongoing evaluations: continuous monitoring and deficiency-remediation tracking
exclusions: Does NOT substitute for the full regulatory control set; this UC provides one of several evidence streams and must be combined with policy, procedure, and supplemental UCs to demonstrate the complete clause.
evidence: Scheduled search output persisted to the compliance summary index with regulation=COSO, clause=Principle16, available as a saved search and exportable as a CSV evidence pack for the responsible officer.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Phase-C authored UC targeting COSO Principle 16 (Ongoing and/or separate evaluations). Monitors continuous-monitoring programme completeness and deficiency remediation; assurance level set to 'contributing' pending SME review per Phase E of the regulation-coverage plan.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that COSO Principle 16 (Ongoing and/or separate evaluations) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.50.34: COSO Principle 16 — Ongoing evaluations: continuous monitoring and deficiency-remediation tracking.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.50.34' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.
compliance.smeCaveat: Phase-C draft: SME review required before uplifting assurance from 'contributing' to 'partial' or 'full'. Confirm that the SPL captures the specific regulator expectation and that the telemetry exists in the deployment.

dataSources: Internal-audit programme schedules, control-deficiency trackers, remediation ticketing systems, continuous-monitoring dashboards.
spl:
index=grc_compliance sourcetype IN (monitoring_programme, control_deficiency) earliest=-90d
| eval deficiency_remediated=if(status IN ("remediated", "closed"), 1, 0)
| eval evaluation_completed=if(sourcetype="monitoring_programme" AND eval_status="completed", 1, 0)
| stats count AS total_items sum(deficiency_remediated) AS remediated sum(evaluation_completed) AS evaluations_done dc(control_area) AS areas_covered avg(eval(if(deficiency_remediated=1, round((resolution_time - _time)/86400), null()))) AS avg_remediation_days BY programme, quarter
| eval remediation_pct=round(100*remediated/total_items, 1)
| where remediation_pct<100 OR evaluations_done=0
| table programme, quarter, total_items, remediated, remediation_pct, evaluations_done, areas_covered, avg_remediation_days
description: Tracks the continuous-monitoring programme's evaluation completeness and deficiency-remediation rates per COSO Principle 16, ensuring ongoing and separate evaluations are performed and findings are resolved.
value: COSO Principle 16 requires the entity to select, develop, and perform ongoing and/or separate evaluations. Monitoring programme coverage and deficiency resolution provides auditable evidence of evaluation rigour.
implementation: (1) Ingest monitoring-programme schedules and control-deficiency events; (2) schedule quarterly; (3) report to audit committee. Indexes required: `index=grc_compliance`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=control_deficiency`, `sourcetype=monitoring_programme`.
visualization: Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner. Key fields: `areas_covered`, `avg_remediation_days`, `deficiency_remediated`, `evaluation_completed`, `evaluations_done`, `programme`.
knownFalsePositives: Tuning prerequisites: source field mappings must be in place before the UC will produce reliable metrics; early runs will commonly flag unmapped systems until onboarding is completed.
grandmaExplanation: We keep track of tracks the continuous-monitoring programme's evaluation completeness and deficiency-remediation rates per coso principle 16, ensuring ongoing and separate evaluations are performed and findings are. This helps maintain steady compliance posture and reduces manual review effort.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Internal-audit programme schedules, control-deficiency trackers, remediation ticketing systems, continuous-monitoring dashboards.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Ingest monitoring-programme schedules and control-deficiency events; (2) schedule quarterly; (3) report to audit committee.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=grc_compliance sourcetype IN (monitoring_programme, control_deficiency) earliest=-90d
| eval deficiency_remediated=if(status IN ("remediated", "closed"), 1, 0)
| eval evaluation_completed=if(sourcetype="monitoring_programme" AND eval_status="completed", 1, 0)
| stats count AS total_items sum(deficiency_remediated) AS remediated sum(evaluation_completed) AS evaluations_done dc(control_area) AS areas_covered avg(eval(if(deficiency_remediated=1, round((resolution_time - _time)/86400), null()))) AS avg_remediation_days BY programme, quarter
| eval remediation_pct=round(100*remediated/total_items, 1)
| where remediation_pct<100 OR evaluations_done=0
| table programme, quarter, total_items, remediated, remediation_pct, evaluations_done, areas_covered, avg_remediation_days
```

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner.
